profile.name: Yuvan Sankar
profile.tagline: I build AI-powered products — from RAG systems to real-time interview platforms.
profile.bio: I build real-world applications at the intersection of AI, web, and systems — RAG tools that answer questions from video, and platforms that run live technical interviews. I'm a Computer & Communication Engineering student who enjoys solving logical challenges and shipping things people actually use. Alongside development, I've co-authored two IEEE conference papers on secure, privacy-preserving IoT.

experience.summary: An AI internship focused on applied concepts, machine learning, and AI-driven problem solving.
highlights: Built Eco Pack AI — an AI-based approach to smart, sustainable packaging solutions.
Gained hands-on experience with AI tools, model understanding, and real-world implementation.
Explored AI/ML workflows end-to-end, from concept to working prototype.

projects.title: YapBack
projects.subtitle: AI-Powered YouTube Video Q&A
projects.description: A RAG application that answers questions from any YouTube video with transcript-grounded, timestamp-cited responses. Semantic search runs over Gemini embeddings with HNSW vector indexing, wrapped in a Streamlit chat interface with clickable deep-links.

projects.title: CrackItNow
projects.subtitle: AI Technical Interview Platform
projects.description: A mock-interview platform that generates technical questions and evaluates candidate responses with the Gemini API. Real-time coding via Monaco Editor and Judge0, JWT auth, WebSocket communication, and text-to-speech for an interactive interview simulation.

projects.title: Eco Pack AI
projects.subtitle: Sustainable Packaging Intelligence
projects.description: Built during my Infosys AI internship — an AI-driven approach to smart, sustainable packaging solutions, exploring model understanding and real-world implementation.

projects.title: CodeGenAgent
projects.subtitle: Autonomous Coding Agent
projects.description: An experimental TypeScript agent that generates and edits code from natural-language instructions — exploring how far a structured agent loop can carry a task on its own.

publications.title: Quantum-Resilient and Privacy-Preserving AIoT: A Secure Edge Intelligence Framework
publications.venue: IEEE Conference · D. Y. Patil University, Pune

publications.title: Lightweight Blockchain Framework for Secure and Privacy-Preserving IoT Systems
publications.venue: IEEE Conference · Vidyavardhaka College of Engineering, Mysuru
